summary: Misrepresented package silently loads unrelated scoped dependency on require()
details: Package `@sql-access/nodesql` presents three inconsistent identities: the package name advertises a SQL-access library, the README describes itself as `bare-stream` ("Streaming data for JavaScript"), and `index.js` is a verbatim copy of the unrelated `buffer` module by Feross Aboukhadijeh. Inserted at the top of `index.js` (line 11) is a dynamic import statement `var ins = import('@sqlite-node/createsql');` that fires unconditionally whenever a consumer calls `require('@sql-access/nodesql')`. The imported `@sqlite-node/createsql` is a separate scoped package whose code is fetched from the registry at require time and is not part of this tarball, so its behavior cannot be audited from this package. The combination of identity misrepresentation (name/README/code disagree) and a hidden top-level load of an unrelated sibling-scope package is the namespace-abuse / dropper shape: any installer that requires this package for SQL functionality instead loads buffer plus an opaque second package selected by the publisher.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
